steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $\frac{x{y}^{2}+11xy-7y}{{x}^{2}y-7xy}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Factor the second denominator
steps.content: Start with $$\frac{y+10}{x-7}+\frac{y(x-7)}{x^2y-7xy}$$ The second denominator factors as $$x^2y-7xy=xy(x-7)$$ so the first fraction needs to be multiplied by $\frac{xy}{xy}$ to get the same denominator.

steps.title: Combine the numerators
steps.content: With the common denominator, the expression becomes $$\frac{xy(y+10)}{xy(x-7)}+\frac{y(x-7)}{xy(x-7)}$$ Combine the numerators: $$\frac{xy(y+10)+y(x-7)}{xy(x-7)}$$ Distribute in the numerator: $$xy^2+10xy+xy-7y=xy^2+11xy-7y$$

steps.title: Match the final form
steps.content: The denominator $xy(x-7)$ is the same as $x^2y-7xy$, so the simplified expression is $$\frac{xy^2+11xy-7y}{x^2y-7xy}$$ That matches Choice C — $\frac{x{y}^{2}+11xy-7y}{{x}^{2}y-7xy}$.
